name: Active Directory / Kerberos Attacks
detection: Windows Security Event logs (SIEM), AD audit logs, EDR, Honey accounts
scenario: Suspicious Kerberos activity is detected indicating potential Kerberoasting, AS-REP Roasting, Pass-the-Ticket, Golden Ticket, or DCSync attack. Indicators include excessive TGS requests for service accounts (Event 4769 with RC4 encryption), pre-authentication failures (4768 error 0x18), logons using forged tickets, or non-DC machines requesting AD replication (Event 4662).
tools: SIEM (Splunk, Sentinel); Windows Security Event logs; AD audit logs; EDR (CrowdStrike, Defender); BloodHound/SharpHound (for AD path analysis); Velociraptor

detSteps.title: Detect Kerberoasting - excessive TGS-REQ with RC4 encryption
detSteps.detail: Kerberoasting requests Kerberos service tickets for accounts with SPNs using RC4 (0x17) encryption rather than AES, so the ticket can be cracked offline. Look for Event ID 4769 (Kerberos Service Ticket Operations) with Ticket Encryption Type 0x17 (RC4-HMAC) from a single account in a short window, targeting multiple service accounts. Normal environments use AES (0x12 or 0x11).

queries.splunk: index=wineventlog EventCode=4769 Ticket_Encryption_Type=0x17 NOT ServiceName="krbtgt" NOT ServiceName=*$ | stats count as tgs_requests dc(ServiceName) as unique_spns by Account_Name, Client_Address | where tgs_requests > 5 | sort -tgs_requests
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where EventID == 4769
| where TicketEncryptionType == '0x17'
| where ServiceName !endswith '$' and ServiceName != 'krbtgt'
| summarize TGSCount=count(), UniqueSPNs=dcount(ServiceName), SPNList=make_set(ServiceName) by Account, IpAddress
| where TGSCount > 5
| order by TGSCount desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username as account, sourceip, COUNT(*) as tgs_count, COUNT(DISTINCT "Service Name") as unique_spns FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) = 'Microsoft Windows Security Event Log' AND "Event ID" = '4769' AND "Ticket Encryption Type" = '0x17' AND "Service Name" NOT ILIKE '%$' GROUP BY username, sourceip HAVING tgs_count > 5 ORDER BY tgs_count DESC LAST 1 HOURS
queries.sigma:
title: Kerberoasting via RC4 Downgrade
status: stable
description: Detects Kerberoasting - multiple TGS requests with RC4 encryption targeting service accounts
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 4769
    TicketEncryptionType: '0x17'
  filter_machine_accounts:
    ServiceName|endswith: '$'
  filter_krbtgt:
    ServiceName: 'krbtgt'
  condition: selection and not filter_machine_accounts and not filter_krbtgt
falsepositives:
  - Legacy systems that only support RC4
level: high
queries.velociraptor:
SELECT EventID, TimeGenerated, Computer, AccountName, ServiceName, TicketEncryptionType, ClientAddress
FROM wmi(query="SELECT * FROM Win32_NTLogEvent WHERE Logfile='Security' AND EventCode=4769")
WHERE TicketEncryptionType = '0x17'
AND NOT ServiceName =~ '.*\\$'
AND ServiceName != 'krbtgt'
ORDER BY TimeGenerated DESC
LIMIT 200
queries.carbon_black: (process_name:Rubeus.exe OR process_name:GetUserSPNs.exe OR cmdline:"kerberoast") OR (process_name:powershell.exe AND cmdline:"Get-DomainSPNTicket")
queries.security_onion:
# Security Onion KQL - Active Directory / Kerberos Attacks (step 1)
# Ransomware precursors: VSS deletion, rapid file modification, ransom notes
# Shadow copy deletion
event.code:1 AND (process.name:vssadmin.exe AND process.command_line:*delete*)
OR (process.name:bcdedit.exe AND process.command_line:*norecoveryenabled*)
OR (process.name:wbadmin.exe AND process.command_line:*delete*)

# Ransom note file creation
event.module:sysmon AND event.code:11
  AND (file.name:*README* OR file.name:*DECRYPT* OR file.name:*RECOVER*
       OR file.name:*.locked OR file.name:*.encrypted OR file.name:*.crypt)

# Lateral SMB propagation
event.dataset:zeek.conn AND destination.port:445
  AND NOT (source.ip:10.0.0.0/8 OR source.ip:172.16.0.0/12 OR source.ip:192.168.0.0/16)
queries.osquery:
-- OSQuery: Active Directory / Kerberos Attacks (step 1)
-- Check for high file-write processes (ransomware encryption activity)
SELECT pid, name, path, cmdline FROM processes
WHERE name IN ('vssadmin.exe','bcdedit.exe','wbadmin.exe','diskpart.exe')
   OR cmdline LIKE '%delete shadow%' OR cmdline LIKE '%norecoveryenabled%';

-- Find ransom note / encrypted file artifacts (last 1 hour)
SELECT path, size, mtime FROM file
WHERE (filename LIKE '%.locked' OR filename LIKE '%.encrypted' OR filename LIKE '%.crypt'
       OR filename LIKE '%README%' OR filename LIKE '%DECRYPT%')
  AND mtime > (strftime('%s','now') - 3600);

-- VSS shadow copy status
SELECT * FROM shadow_copies WHERE volume IS NOT NULL;

detSteps.title: Detect AS-REP Roasting - pre-authentication disabled accounts
detSteps.detail: AS-REP Roasting targets accounts where Kerberos pre-authentication is disabled (DONT_REQ_PREAUTH flag set). The attacker requests a TGT for such an account without knowing the password and receives an encrypted TGT that can be cracked offline. Look for Event 4768 with error code 0x0 (success) for accounts that do not normally receive TGT requests, or multiple 4768 requests to the same user from unusual client addresses.

queries.splunk: index=wineventlog EventCode=4768 Pre_Authentication_Type=0 | stats count by TargetUserName, Client_Address | where count > 1 | sort -count
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where EventID == 4768
| where PreAuthType == '0'
| summarize RequestCount=count() by TargetUserName, IpAddress
| where RequestCount > 1
| order by RequestCount desc
queries.qradar: SELECT "Target Username" as target, sourceip, COUNT(*) as req_count FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) = 'Microsoft Windows Security Event Log' AND "Event ID" = '4768' AND "Pre-Authentication Type" = '0' GROUP BY "Target Username", sourceip HAVING req_count > 1 ORDER BY req_count DESC LAST 1 HOURS
queries.sigma:
title: AS-REP Roasting - Pre-Authentication Disabled
status: stable
description: Detects AS-REP requests against accounts with Kerberos pre-authentication disabled
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 4768
    PreAuthType: '0'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Service accounts explicitly configured without pre-auth
level: high
queries.velociraptor:
SELECT EventID, TimeGenerated, Computer, TargetUserName, PreAuthType, ClientAddress
FROM wmi(query="SELECT * FROM Win32_NTLogEvent WHERE Logfile='Security' AND EventCode=4768")
WHERE PreAuthType = '0'
ORDER BY TimeGenerated DESC
LIMIT 200
queries.security_onion:
# Security Onion KQL - Active Directory / Kerberos Attacks (step 2)
# Ransomware precursors: VSS deletion, rapid file modification, ransom notes
# Shadow copy deletion
event.code:1 AND (process.name:vssadmin.exe AND process.command_line:*delete*)
OR (process.name:bcdedit.exe AND process.command_line:*norecoveryenabled*)
OR (process.name:wbadmin.exe AND process.command_line:*delete*)

# Ransom note file creation
event.module:sysmon AND event.code:11
  AND (file.name:*README* OR file.name:*DECRYPT* OR file.name:*RECOVER*
       OR file.name:*.locked OR file.name:*.encrypted OR file.name:*.crypt)

# Lateral SMB propagation
event.dataset:zeek.conn AND destination.port:445
  AND NOT (source.ip:10.0.0.0/8 OR source.ip:172.16.0.0/12 OR source.ip:192.168.0.0/16)
queries.osquery:
-- OSQuery: Active Directory / Kerberos Attacks (step 2)
-- Check for high file-write processes (ransomware encryption activity)
SELECT pid, name, path, cmdline FROM processes
WHERE name IN ('vssadmin.exe','bcdedit.exe','wbadmin.exe','diskpart.exe')
   OR cmdline LIKE '%delete shadow%' OR cmdline LIKE '%norecoveryenabled%';

-- Find ransom note / encrypted file artifacts (last 1 hour)
SELECT path, size, mtime FROM file
WHERE (filename LIKE '%.locked' OR filename LIKE '%.encrypted' OR filename LIKE '%.crypt'
       OR filename LIKE '%README%' OR filename LIKE '%DECRYPT%')
  AND mtime > (strftime('%s','now') - 3600);

-- VSS shadow copy status
SELECT * FROM shadow_copies WHERE volume IS NOT NULL;

detSteps.title: Detect Golden / Silver Ticket and Pass-the-Ticket
detSteps.detail: Golden and Silver tickets are forged Kerberos tickets that allow attackers to authenticate as any user (Golden) or to specific services (Silver) without valid credentials. Indicators: Event 4769 with ticket lifetime > 10 hours (Golden Ticket default is 10y), logons using tickets from accounts that never had an interactive session, or Event 4624 (LogonType 3 or 9) for accounts that do not match expected patterns. Pass-the-Ticket: Event 4768/4769 with client address not matching the logon workstation.

queries.splunk: index=wineventlog EventCode=4769 | eval ticket_life=Ticket_Options | stats count by Account_Name, ServiceName, Client_Address, Ticket_Options | where isnotnull(Ticket_Options) | sort -count
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(4h)
| where EventID == 4769
| extend TicketLifeHours = toint(TicketOptions)
| where Account !endswith '$'
| summarize count() by Account, ServiceName, IpAddress, Computer
| where count_ > 20
| order by count_ desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, "Service Name", sourceip, "Ticket Options", QIDNAME(qid) as event FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) = 'Microsoft Windows Security Event Log' AND "Event ID" IN ('4768','4769','4624') AND magnitude > 3 ORDER BY magnitude DESC LAST 4 HOURS
queries.sigma:
title: Golden Ticket - Abnormal Kerberos TGT Usage
status: experimental
description: Detects potential Golden Ticket usage via anomalous Kerberos TGT characteristics
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 4769
    TicketOptions: '0x40810010'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative Kerberos operations
level: critical
queries.velociraptor:
SELECT Name, Pid, Username, CreateTime
FROM pslist()
WHERE Name =~ '(?i)(mimikatz|sekurlsa|kerberos)'
ORDER BY CreateTime DESC
queries.carbon_black: (process_name:mimikatz.exe OR cmdline:"kerberos::golden" OR cmdline:"kerberos::silver" OR cmdline:"sekurlsa::tickets")
queries.security_onion:
# Security Onion KQL - Active Directory / Kerberos Attacks (step 3)
# Ransomware precursors: VSS deletion, rapid file modification, ransom notes
# Shadow copy deletion
event.code:1 AND (process.name:vssadmin.exe AND process.command_line:*delete*)
OR (process.name:bcdedit.exe AND process.command_line:*norecoveryenabled*)
OR (process.name:wbadmin.exe AND process.command_line:*delete*)

# Ransom note file creation
event.module:sysmon AND event.code:11
  AND (file.name:*README* OR file.name:*DECRYPT* OR file.name:*RECOVER*
       OR file.name:*.locked OR file.name:*.encrypted OR file.name:*.crypt)

# Lateral SMB propagation
event.dataset:zeek.conn AND destination.port:445
  AND NOT (source.ip:10.0.0.0/8 OR source.ip:172.16.0.0/12 OR source.ip:192.168.0.0/16)
queries.osquery:
-- OSQuery: Active Directory / Kerberos Attacks (step 3)
-- Check for high file-write processes (ransomware encryption activity)
SELECT pid, name, path, cmdline FROM processes
WHERE name IN ('vssadmin.exe','bcdedit.exe','wbadmin.exe','diskpart.exe')
   OR cmdline LIKE '%delete shadow%' OR cmdline LIKE '%norecoveryenabled%';

-- Find ransom note / encrypted file artifacts (last 1 hour)
SELECT path, size, mtime FROM file
WHERE (filename LIKE '%.locked' OR filename LIKE '%.encrypted' OR filename LIKE '%.crypt'
       OR filename LIKE '%README%' OR filename LIKE '%DECRYPT%')
  AND mtime > (strftime('%s','now') - 3600);

-- VSS shadow copy status
SELECT * FROM shadow_copies WHERE volume IS NOT NULL;

detSteps.title: Detect DCSync attack - unauthorised AD replication
detSteps.detail: DCSync abuses the MS-DRSR replication protocol to pull password hashes from AD without code execution on the DC. The attacker's workstation impersonates a domain controller and requests replication. Indicators: Event 4662 on a DC with Access Mask 0x100 and GUID {1131f6aa-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2} (DS-Replication-Get-Changes-All) from a non-DC machine. Also check for mimikatz lsadump::dcsync in process command lines.

queries.splunk: index=wineventlog EventCode=4662 ObjectType="{19195a5b-6da0-11d0-afd3-00c04fd930c9}" NOT SubjectDomainName IN ("NT AUTHORITY","BUILTIN") | stats count by SubjectUserName, SubjectLogonId, ComputerName | sort -count
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(4h)
| where EventID == 4662
| where ObjectType contains '19195a5b-6da0-11d0-afd3-00c04fd930c9'
| where SubjectAccount !endswith '$'
| where SubjectDomainName !in ('NT AUTHORITY','BUILTIN')
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, SubjectAccount, SubjectLogonId, ObjectName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, sourceip, "Object Type" as objtype, "Access Mask" as mask, QIDNAME(qid) as event FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) = 'Microsoft Windows Security Event Log' AND "Event ID" = '4662' AND "Access Mask" IN ('0x100','0x40000') ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 4 HOURS
queries.sigma:
title: DCSync via Replication Rights Abuse
status: stable
description: Detects DCSync attack using replication privileges from a non-domain-controller
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 4662
    AccessMask: '0x100'
    ObjectType|contains: '19195a5b-6da0-11d0-afd3-00c04fd930c9'
  filter_dc:
    SubjectUserName|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection and not filter_dc
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate AD replication tools run by domain admins
level: critical
queries.velociraptor:
SELECT Name, Pid, CommandLine, Username, CreateTime
FROM pslist()
WHERE CommandLine =~ '(?i)(lsadump::dcsync|dcsync|drsuapi)'
ORDER BY CreateTime DESC
queries.security_onion:
# Security Onion KQL - Active Directory / Kerberos Attacks (step 4)
# Ransomware precursors: VSS deletion, rapid file modification, ransom notes
# Shadow copy deletion
event.code:1 AND (process.name:vssadmin.exe AND process.command_line:*delete*)
OR (process.name:bcdedit.exe AND process.command_line:*norecoveryenabled*)
OR (process.name:wbadmin.exe AND process.command_line:*delete*)

# Ransom note file creation
event.module:sysmon AND event.code:11
  AND (file.name:*README* OR file.name:*DECRYPT* OR file.name:*RECOVER*
       OR file.name:*.locked OR file.name:*.encrypted OR file.name:*.crypt)

# Lateral SMB propagation
event.dataset:zeek.conn AND destination.port:445
  AND NOT (source.ip:10.0.0.0/8 OR source.ip:172.16.0.0/12 OR source.ip:192.168.0.0/16)
queries.osquery:
-- OSQuery: Active Directory / Kerberos Attacks (step 4)
-- Check for high file-write processes (ransomware encryption activity)
SELECT pid, name, path, cmdline FROM processes
WHERE name IN ('vssadmin.exe','bcdedit.exe','wbadmin.exe','diskpart.exe')
   OR cmdline LIKE '%delete shadow%' OR cmdline LIKE '%norecoveryenabled%';

-- Find ransom note / encrypted file artifacts (last 1 hour)
SELECT path, size, mtime FROM file
WHERE (filename LIKE '%.locked' OR filename LIKE '%.encrypted' OR filename LIKE '%.crypt'
       OR filename LIKE '%README%' OR filename LIKE '%DECRYPT%')
  AND mtime > (strftime('%s','now') - 3600);

-- VSS shadow copy status
SELECT * FROM shadow_copies WHERE volume IS NOT NULL;

contSteps.title: Isolate the source account and workstation immediately
contSteps.detail: Disable the account used to request RC4 TGS tickets or perform DCSync. Force a password reset for any service account identified in Kerberoasting attempts. Isolate the workstation that sourced the requests using EDR network isolation. Do not simply lock the account if DCSync occurred - all domain hashes may be compromised.

queries.splunk: index=wineventlog EventCode=4769 Ticket_Encryption_Type=0x17 | stats dc(ServiceName) as targets by Client_Address, Account_Name | where targets > 3 | sort -targets
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4769
| where TicketEncryptionType == '0x17'
| summarize SPNTargets=dcount(ServiceName) by IpAddress, Account
| where SPNTargets > 3
| order by SPNTargets desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, sourceip, COUNT(DISTINCT "Service Name") as spn_count FROM events WHERE "Event ID" = '4769' AND "Ticket Encryption Type" = '0x17' GROUP BY username, sourceip HAVING spn_count > 3 ORDER BY spn_count DESC LAST 1 HOURS
queries.velociraptor: SELECT Laddr, Lport, Raddr, Rport, State, Pid, Name FROM netstat() WHERE Rport IN (389, 636, 3268, 3269, 88, 135, 445) ORDER BY Rport
queries.carbon_black: hostname:<attacker_host> AND (process_name:Rubeus.exe OR process_name:mimikatz.exe OR cmdline:kerberoast)

contSteps.title: Reset krbtgt password twice if Golden Ticket is suspected
contSteps.detail: If a Golden Ticket is confirmed or strongly suspected, the krbtgt account password must be reset TWICE (with at least 10-hour gap between resets per Microsoft guidance) to invalidate all outstanding tickets. Coordinate with the AD team. Plan for Kerberos authentication disruption across the domain during resets. Document all service disruptions.

queries.splunk: index=wineventlog EventCode=4723 OR EventCode=4724 TargetUserName=krbtgt | table _time, SubjectUserName, TargetUserName, ComputerName
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID in (4723, 4724)
| where TargetUserName == 'krbtgt'
| project TimeGenerated, SubjectAccount, TargetUserName, Computer
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, "Target Username" as target, QIDNAME(qid) as event, starttime FROM events WHERE "Event ID" IN ('4723','4724') AND "Target Username" = 'krbtgt' ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 24 HOURS

contSteps.title: Force domain-wide re-authentication
contSteps.detail: After krbtgt password resets, all active Kerberos sessions will become invalid. Coordinate with the business to plan a controlled forced re-authentication window. Monitor for authentication errors across the domain. Track recovery of services that use Kerberos-based authentication (IIS, SQL, SharePoint, etc.).

queries.splunk: index=wineventlog EventCode IN (4768, 4769, 4771) | timechart count span=5m by EventCode
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
| where EventID in (4768, 4769, 4771)
| summarize count() by EventID, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT "Event ID", COUNT(*) as auth_events, starttime FROM events WHERE "Event ID" IN ('4768','4769','4771') GROUP BY "Event ID", starttime ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 2 HOURS

eradSteps.title: Remove attacker tooling and persistence from compromised hosts
eradSteps.detail: Hunt for and remove attacker tooling such as Mimikatz, Rubeus, SharpHound, BloodHound, or PowerSploit. Check for PowerShell scripts in common drop locations. Audit scheduled tasks, services, and WMI subscriptions for attacker persistence. Remove any new domain admin accounts or group membership additions.

queries.splunk: index=wineventlog EventCode=4728 OR EventCode=4732 | table _time, SubjectUserName, MemberName, GroupName, ComputerName | sort _time
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where EventID in (4728, 4732, 4756)
| project TimeGenerated, SubjectAccount, MemberName, TargetUserName, Computer
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, "Member Name" as member, "Group Name" as grp, QIDNAME(qid) as event FROM events WHERE "Event ID" IN ('4728','4732','4756') ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 24 HOURS
queries.velociraptor:
SELECT Key, Value, Mtime FROM read_reg_key(glob='HKLM/SYSTEM/CurrentControlSet/Services/*')
WHERE Mtime > now() - 86400
ORDER BY Mtime DESC
queries.carbon_black: (process_name:mimikatz.exe OR process_name:Rubeus.exe OR process_name:SharpHound.exe OR cmdline:"Invoke-BloodHound" OR cmdline:"Invoke-Mimikatz")

eradSteps.title: Audit and remediate over-privileged accounts and SPNs
eradSteps.detail: Review all service accounts with SPNs and remove unnecessary ones. Enforce AES-only Kerberos (disable RC4) via GPO (Network security: Configure encryption types for Kerberos). Implement tiered admin model. Enable Protected Users security group for all privileged accounts. Audit for accounts with DONT_REQ_PREAUTH enabled and remove the flag unless absolutely required.

queries.splunk: index=wineventlog EventCode=4769 | stats dc(ServiceName) as spn_count by Account_Name | sort -spn_count | head 20
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4769
| where TicketEncryptionType == '0x17'
| summarize SPNCount=dcount(ServiceName), RecentRequests=count() by Account
| order by SPNCount desc
| take 20
queries.qradar: SELECT username, COUNT(DISTINCT "Service Name") as spn_count FROM events WHERE "Event ID" = '4769' GROUP BY username ORDER BY spn_count DESC LAST 7 DAYS

recSteps.title: Restore verified-clean domain controllers from backup if required
recSteps.detail: If DCSync confirmed domain-wide credential compromise or if DCs were directly compromised, consider rebuilding DCs from known-good snapshots. Restore to an isolated network segment, verify integrity, then reconnect. All AD objects must be reviewed for attacker-introduced changes (new accounts, modified ACLs, new GPOs).

queries.splunk: index=wineventlog EventCode=4720 OR EventCode=4722 | table _time, SubjectUserName, TargetUserName, ComputerName | sort _time
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(72h)
| where EventID in (4720, 4722, 4738)
| project TimeGenerated, SubjectAccount, TargetUserName, Computer
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, "Target Username" as new_account, QIDNAME(qid) as event FROM events WHERE "Event ID" IN ('4720','4722','4738') ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 72 HOURS

recSteps.title: Monitor intensively for 30 days post-incident
recSteps.detail: Kerberos attacks often indicate a prolonged dwell time. After eradication, monitor for recurrence of RC4 TGS requests, new SPN creation, AD replication events from non-DCs, and lateral movement. Alert on any new domain admin account creation or group membership changes. Maintain elevated alert priority for 30 days.

queries.splunk: index=wineventlog EventCode IN (4769, 4662, 4728) | timechart count span=1h by EventCode
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID in (4769, 4662, 4728, 4720)
| summarize count() by EventID, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT "Event ID", COUNT(*) as count, starttime FROM events WHERE "Event ID" IN ('4769','4662','4728','4720') GROUP BY "Event ID", starttime ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 7 DAYS

metrics.name: Detection Time
metrics.target: <15 minutes from first RC4 TGS request

metrics.name: krbtgt Reset
metrics.target: Within 1 hour if Golden Ticket confirmed
